prompt_summary: Create task_1_2.ipynb documenting 3+ method-specific assumptions with violation scenarios and paper references

interaction_details.user_request: Create notebook identifying 3+ key assumptions the paper makes, formatted as Assumption/Why/Violation/Reference for each
interaction_details.assistant_approach: Generated markdown notebook with three carefully chosen assumptions: (1) Latent variables exist and are predictable from features (H-step stability), (2) Loss function decomposes over latent values (CCCP decomposition), (3) Local optima from CCCP generalize well. Each includes violation scenarios and paper citations.
key_components: Three well-justified assumptions derived from paper's theory
Concrete violation scenarios for testing later
interaction_details.llm_tool_use: create_file tool to generate .ipynb with markdown content
assumptions_identified: Assumption 1: Latent variables exist and predictable from features
Assumption 2: Loss function decomposes over latent values
Assumption 3: Local optima from CCCP generalize well

disclosure_statement: This notebook was generated by Claude Haiku 4.5 to extract and document assumptions from the paper. The three assumptions are derived from careful reading of Section 3 (CCCP algorithm) and Section 5 (ranking application). Violation scenarios are original contributions of the LLM to aid understanding.
top_5_prompts: Create notebook identifying 3+ key assumptions the paper makes, formatted as Assumption/Why/Violation/Reference for each
What counts as a method-specific assumption vs. a general ML assumption?
How can I identify assumptions that don't appear explicitly in the paper but are necessary for the method to work?
Should violation scenarios be realistic or extreme to best test the method?
How do I tie assumptions to specific paper sections for future reference in Part C?

declaration.declaration_text: I declare that the above information is accurate and complete. All LLM interactions documented were used for brainstorming assumptions and understanding assumption types. No code was generated by LLM for Task 1.2. All assumptions are identified and explained in my own words based on careful reading of the paper.
